authoritative_sources.title: Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods and related FDA Food Traceability Rule guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary U.S. authority for food traceability requirements. This guidance describes the Food Traceability Rule, the covered traceability records, and the critical tracking events that retail-facing CPG operators must preserve for certain foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability-aware intake and evidence sufficiency review

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Intends to Extend Compliance Date for Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is FDA's authoritative public update dated March 20, 2025 stating that FDA intended to extend the Food Traceability Rule compliance date by 30 months; that date context matters for current deployment and operator expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: deployment and timing posture for food-traceability controls

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the governing public rule set for food CGMP, warehousing and distribution, recall plans, record retention, and supply-chain controls that constrain release and hold decisions in food CPG operations.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 7 Subpart C - Recalls (Including Product Corrections)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 7 Subpart C is the authoritative FDA recall-policy baseline for handling product corrections and recalls after distribution and defines where routine execution must stop and formal recall ownership begins.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: post-distribution hold, correction, and recall boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA guidance on voluntary recall initiation is an authoritative public operating reference for timely recall action, evidence expectations, and coordination steps once a distributed product issue is identified.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail and CPG identification standards. The General Specifications are the core public standard for GTIN, GLN, and barcode usage across trading partners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item, pack, and location identity normalisation

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's Global Traceability Standard is the recognized cross-industry standard for interoperable traceability, including critical tracking events, key data elements, and the identify-capture-share model across trading partners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory, shipment, and lot traceability reconciliation

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Data Model and Global Data Synchronisation Network
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 GDM and GDSN are authoritative vendor-neutral workflow references for the product, pack, and attribute data needed to list, order, store, move, and sell CPG items between brand owners and retailers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: product data, pricing, and listing prerequisite validation

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, retailer, store or DC, product family, GTIN, order, promotion, and service-level context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Item, pricing, and order prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify customer-item setup, pack hierarchy, price, target market, promotion dates, and retailer order references so execution does not proceed on stale or incomplete product data.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Inventory and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review ERP, OMS, WMS, ASN, retailer receiving, and lot records to determine whether order, shipment, and receipt status can safely advance.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Quality hold, release, and recall boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve quality holds, recall posture, and release boundaries whenever food-safety, nonconformance, or distributed-product concerns affect retail execution.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework progression
workflow_stages.description: Move short ships, pricing defects, deduction risks, shelf-set failures, and missing evidence into explicit blocked or rework states with owner routing instead of silent closure.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when proof-of-execution evidence exists and any tenant, retailer, or franchise controls that are stricter than the public baseline have been applied.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: retailer purchase orders, customer-item setup records, GTIN and case-pack hierarchies
price lists, promotion calendars, planograms, and shelf-set instructions
ERP order status, OMS acknowledgements, ASN, shipment confirmations, and proof-of-delivery records
WMS inventory states, lot and batch genealogy, retailer receiving logs, and store execution reports
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, recall notices, and change-control records
supplier records, approved-item and approved-supplier lists, and service-level commitments
deduction, chargeback, and franchise exception notes
systems_of_record: retailer portal or EDI integration records

allowed: Classify retail execution requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, inventory, lot, quality, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires quality release, recall ownership, supplier approval, financial approval, or commercial concession authority.
A retailer, distributor, or franchise policy materially conflicts with the public baseline.
A quality hold, recall concern, supplier substitution request, or traceability conflict changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Approve supplier substitutions, alternate items, or release overrides without documented authority.
Bypass traceability, release, quality, pricing, or inventory controls because of urgency or customer pressure.

domain_failure_modes: inventory, ASN, and retailer receiving records conflict and the lane closes the order anyway
a held or nonconforming lot is shipped to protect a promotion window
customer-item, GTIN, or price data are incomplete and execution proceeds on the wrong product or price
supplier substitution is treated as an execution convenience rather than an approval-bound decision
field or shelf execution is marked complete without auditable proof of execution
food-safety or recall guidance is overgeneralized to nonfood categories without tenant or product-category grounding
retail execution drifts into commercial, quality, or supplier-approval authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Consumer packaged goods spans food, beverage, household, cosmetics, OTC, and other categories with different regulator-specific release and recall obligations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FDA food-traceability and recall sources plus GS1 cross-category retail standards as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant-supplied product-category, jurisdiction, and facility facts before applying category-specific regulated interpretations outside the shared baseline.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only food-safety policy.

The lane stops at operator-side retail execution. Formal quality release, supplier approval, and commercial concession authority stay outside the lane even when the issue appears in an execution queue.
